seoTitle: How to Use the n8n Edit Fields (Set) Node
metaDescription: Learn how to add, update, or remove data fields in n8n using the Edit Fields (Set) node, including working with expressions and JSON.
intro: The Edit Fields node, often still called Set, is one of the most commonly used building blocks in n8n for shaping data as it flows through a workflow. This guide covers adding the node, choosing an operation, and mapping dynamic values so your data comes out structured the way downstream nodes expect.

steps.heading: Add the Edit Fields node
steps.body: Drop the Edit Fields (Set) node onto your workflow canvas where you need to reshape incoming data.

steps.heading: Choose your operation
steps.body: Select whether you want to add, update, or clear fields from the incoming payload.

steps.heading: Pick your data type
steps.body: Set each field's type, such as string, number, or boolean, to match what downstream nodes expect.

steps.heading: Map dynamic values
steps.body: Use the expression editor to pull values from upstream nodes instead of typing static data.

steps.heading: Preview and test
steps.body: Check the data preview pane to confirm your fields are structured correctly before running the full workflow.

faq.question: What is the Edit Fields (Set) node used for in n8n?
faq.answer: It's used to add, update, or remove fields in the data passing through your workflow.

faq.question: Can I switch between visual fields and raw JSON in the Set node?
faq.answer: Yes, the node lets you toggle between individual field inputs and a raw JSON editing mode.

faq.question: How do I reference data from a previous node?
faq.answer: You can use the built-in expression editor to map values dynamically from upstream nodes.

faq.question: Can the Edit Fields node remove data I don't need?
faq.answer: Yes, you can configure it to keep only specific fields, effectively filtering out ones you don't want passed along.
